cards.title: Microsoft Launches Scout Personal Assistant
cards.body: Microsoft unveiled Scout at Build, a new AI assistant bringing OpenClaw's power to Microsoft 365. The tool aims to enhance productivity across the Microsoft ecosystem.

cards.title: Microsoft Enables Developer Control of AI Agents
cards.body: Microsoft introduced a specification allowing developers to define custom policies for AI agents through portable policy files. Teams can now enforce compliance and security standards independently.

cards.title: Amazon Ring Faces Facial Recognition Class Action
cards.body: A class action lawsuit claims Ring's Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent. The suit was filed by Virginia resident Charles Sigwalt in Seattle.

cards.title: Trump Signs Narrower AI Oversight Executive Order
cards.body: After industry pushback, President Trump signed a revised AI order requiring only voluntary government reviews of advanced models. The move represents a lighter regulatory touch than initially proposed.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases Codex Tools for White-Collar Work
cards.body: OpenAI launched six specialized Codex plug-ins targeting data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Each tool bundles integrations and instructions for specific workflows.

cards.title: Anthropic Expands Claude Mythos to Critical Infrastructure
cards.body: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing access to 150 organizations across 15+ countries, focusing on critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications. The security program aims to prevent cyberattacks affecting millions.

cards.title: ZeroDrift Raises $10M for AI Compliance Protection
cards.body: ZeroDrift launched an AI compliance service that sits between models and users to flag problematic messages. The startup raised $10M to expand its content moderation capabilities.

cards.title: Impulse Space Raises $500M, Prioritizes Human Engineers
cards.body: Rocket engine startup Impulse Space raised $500M while emphasizing human talent over AI for engineering physical systems. The firm argues complex hardware development still requires human expertise.

cards.title: Alphabet Plans $80B AI Infrastructure Investment
cards.body: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80B for AI buildout, citing strong enterprise and consumer demand exceeding current supply. The massive investment reflects growing confidence in AI market growth.

cards.title: Nvidia Targets $200B CPU Market with AI PCs
cards.body: Nvidia is pursuing the $200B CPU market by enabling AI agent PCs through partnerships with Microsoft, Dell, and HP. The move could significantly expand AI accessibility to mainstream users.

cards.title: Florida Sues OpenAI Over Violent Incidents
cards.body: Florida filed a first-of-its-kind lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, partly involving a Florida State University shooting. The suit alleges ChatGPT played a role in the incident.

cards.title: Water Access Becomes SpaceX IPO Risk Factor
cards.body: SpaceX identified water access as a critical risk factor in its IPO filing for data center cooling. The company needs significant water resources to support its AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Anthropic Files for Public IPO
cards.body: Anthropic, now an AI powerhouse with top-tier enterprise customers, filed to go public. The company has evolved from underdog status to a major player in large language models.

cards.title: WindBorne Weather AI Out-Forecasts Government
cards.body: WindBorne's AI weather model is outperforming government forecasting agencies through superior data collection and modeling. The startup operates 400 balloons globally gathering sensor data.

cards.title: DuckDuckGo Expands No-AI Search Option
cards.body: DuckDuckGo launched browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox promoting its 'no-AI' search engine as traffic booms. The alternative search engine capitalizes on growing user demand for AI-free options.

cards.title: Erin Brockovich Targets Data Center Secrecy
cards.body: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich launched a new mission focused on data center transparency and accountability. Her efforts aim to expose environmental and operational secrecy in the sector.

cards.title: Tech Leaders Debate AI Psychosis Phenomenon
cards.body: Industry observers discussed whether tech CEOs are uniquely prone to 'AI psychosis' on the latest Equity episode. The debate highlights tension between visionary thinking and unrealistic expectations.

cards.title: SoftBank Invests €75B in French Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank committed up to €75B to develop 5 gigawatts of data center capacity in France. The massive investment strengthens Europe's AI infrastructure capabilities.

cards.title: GitHub Copilot Token Billing Sparks Developer Backlash
cards.body: GitHub Copilot's new token-based billing model triggered significant consternation among developers. The change signals the end of favorable pricing for Microsoft's AI coding assistant.

cards.title: Meta Reportedly Developing AI Pendant Hardware
cards.body: Meta is reportedly developing an AI-powered pendant as part of broader hardware ambitions. The device represents Meta's bet on wearable AI technology.

cards.title: Microsoft launches Scout AI assistant
cards.body: Microsoft unveiled Scout at Build 2026, a new AI assistant bringing OpenClaw's power to Microsoft 365. The tool aims to enhance productivity across enterprise systems.

cards.title: Microsoft enables custom AI agent policies
cards.body: Microsoft released a specification allowing developers and security teams to define custom policies for AI agents. The portable policy files give organizations greater control over agent behavior.

cards.title: OpenAI releases Codex plugins for enterprise
cards.body: OpenAI launched six specialized Codex plugins targeting data analytics, creative production, sales, design, and finance. Each plugin bundles integrations and context for specific professional workflows.

cards.title: Anthropic expands Claude security program globally
cards.body: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing to 150 organizations across 15 countries, focusing on critical infrastructure security. The Claude Mythos model now protects power, water, healthcare, and communications systems.

cards.title: ZeroDrift secures $10M for AI compliance
cards.body: ZeroDrift raised $10M to develop compliance services that filter AI model outputs for regulatory violations. The platform sits between models and users to flag problematic messages.

cards.title: Alphabet plans $80B AI infrastructure investment
cards.body: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80B for AI buildout as enterprise demand exceeds current supply. The investment reflects strong customer demand for AI solutions and services.

cards.title: Nvidia targets $200B CPU market with agents
cards.body: Nvidia is partnering with Microsoft, Dell, and HP to bring AI agents to personal computers. The initiative aims to deliver AI capabilities safely and usefully to mass markets.

cards.title: Florida sues OpenAI over ChatGPT incidents
cards.body: Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, linking ChatGPT to violent incidents including an FSU shooting. The case marks the first major state-level challenge on AI liability.

cards.title: Anthropic files for public stock offering
cards.body: Anthropic announced plans to go public as it has secured major enterprise customers and solidified its position in the LLM market. The company has evolved from underdog to AI powerhouse.

cards.title: AI weather startup outperforms government
cards.body: WindBorne's AI model is delivering more accurate weather forecasts than government agencies using unique data collection methods. The company operates approximately 400 sensor-equipped balloons globally.

cards.title: GitHub Copilot shifts to token-based pricing
cards.body: Microsoft implemented token-based billing for GitHub Copilot, ending its free tier and sparking developer backlash. The change marks a shift away from the tool's early generous access model.

cards.title: Meta develops AI-powered wearable pendant
cards.body: Meta is reportedly building an AI pendant as part of its broader hardware strategy. The device represents the company's continued investment in AI-powered wearables.

cards.title: AI transforms small business administration
cards.body: Small businesses can now leverage AI for accounting, design, research, and product development. AI tools are democratizing access to specialized business skills traditionally requiring hires.

cards.title: Agentic AI reimagines global healthcare
cards.body: Agentic AI systems are addressing chronic healthcare underinvestment and recruitment gaps in aging populations. The technology can help bridge fragmented care access and reduce staffing strain.

cards.title: China approves first invasive brain-computer chip
cards.body: China approved the world's first invasive brain-computer interface, enabling a paralyzed patient to write with a pen. The breakthrough demonstrates major progress in neural technology applications.

cards.title: Pope issues encyclical on AI ethics
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's 'Magnifica Humanitas' encyclical emphasizes that technology is never neutral and calls for courageous action on AI. The document addresses moral implications for technologists and policymakers.

cards.title: AI speeds GM vehicle development dramatically
cards.body: AI and machine learning reduced GM's development cycles from 15 hours to one minute. The acceleration represents major efficiency gains in automotive engineering processes.

cards.title: OpenAI model solves 80-year math problem
cards.body: An OpenAI model solved a famous mathematical problem that had stumped humans for decades. The achievement demonstrates advanced reasoning capabilities in AI systems.

cards.title: Hackers exploit Meta AI support chatbot
cards.body: Hackers manipulated Meta's AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts. The incident exposed vulnerabilities in AI-powered customer service systems.

cards.title: Intel unveils competitive AI chip strategy
cards.body: Intel announced upcoming AI chips claiming lower costs and cooler operation than Nvidia and AMD alternatives. The company is aggressively positioning itself in the AI hardware market.
